title: Decoder bf16 reciprocal normalization datapath
hypothesis: A standalone packed-bf16 reciprocal-normalization datapath can be emitted and measured by RTLGen/OpenROAD so the decoder q8-vs-bf16 normalization frontier can replace the remaining bf16 heuristic gap with physical metrics.

direct_comparison.primary_question: What Nangate45 timing, power, and area does the row-8 bf16 reciprocal-normalization datapath show?
include: packed IEEE bf16 input and output lanes carried as 16-bit vectors
bucketed reciprocal LUT with reciprocal_bits=12 and bucket_shift=4
multiply, rounding, clamp, and bf16 output conversion logic
exclude: full IEEE exception behavior for NaN, infinity, subnormal, or negative inputs
model-quality reruns or distribution robustness claims
choosing q8 over bf16 before the frontier estimator consumes this metric
follow_on_broad_sweep: update the decoder q8 normalization frontier estimator to load this bf16 PPA artifact
rerun the quantization outline with q8 reciprocal, q8 exact, and bf16 reciprocal datapath costs separated by comparable dimension
consider a smaller reciprocal LUT or staged datapath if this block is too area-heavy for the target decoder envelope

expected_benefit: replaces the bf16 reciprocal-normalization placeholder with measured PPA
keeps timing, power, and area visible as separate axes for the quantization decision
documents the cost of the reciprocal LUT and conversion path instead of hiding it in a scalar heuristic
risks: the block is a measurement proxy and clamps unsupported IEEE cases
single-row L1 PPA excludes decoder control, SRAM, and full softmax integration effects
the 2.5 ns sweep target is aggressive; the accepted row is best-effort physical PPA, not a timing-closed product macro

required_evaluations.objective: Measure Nangate45 PPA for the row-8 bf16 reciprocal-normalization datapath with the reciprocal LUT memory-size gate enabled.
